title: Sushi Deck — Product Tour
summary: A showcase deck that exercises every block type and slide feature Sushi Deck supports. Neutral content — no client data.

slides.label: Cover
slides.title: Decks as *data*, served by one API
slides.sub: A portable presentation engine — authored as JSON, rendered by the kit, stored once. This deck demos *every* block.

blocks.headline: Words, with *emphasis*
blocks.lede: The *opener* sets up a slide with an eyebrow, a headline, and an optional lede.

blocks.text: A *paragraph* is free-flowing prose. Inline emphasis uses *asterisks*, which render as italic-accent — the same treatment everywhere the kit draws rich text.

items: Bullets take a custom *marker* (this list uses an arrow).
Each item is rich text, so *emphasis* works here too.
Use them for scannable, parallel points.

blocks.text: A *gold* callout draws the eye to the thing that matters most on the slide.
A *wax* callout carries a cautionary or secondary note.
Everything a recipient sees is rendered from the same DeckJson you edit.

slides.label: Runtime + slide options
slides.disclaimer: This slide sets a mark, an index prefix, and this disclaimer — all optional per-slide fields.

items: *Present* — full-screen deck runtime with keyboard nav + overview + presenter view.
*Scroll* — the same slides as a long-form scrollable page.
*Print → PDF* — export the exact rendered output.
*Theme* — brand tokens restyle every block (moye renders these in Mondrian navy + gold).

slides.label: Getting started
slides.disclaimer: Sample deck — edit or replace it in the admin editor.

blocks.text: Open the admin editor to change this deck, generate a new one from a brief, or seed real content as DeckJson.
Decks stay *owner-scoped* — each consumer only ever sees its own.
